text:
To find out how much more money Dora needs, we first need to calculate the total cost of the items she wants to buy and then subtract the money she already has.

The total cost of the skipping rope, board game, and playground ball is:
\[ \text{Skipping rope: } \$6 \]
\[ \text{Board game: } \$11 \]
\[ \text{Playground ball: } \$2 \]
\[ \text{Total cost: } \$6 + \$11 + \$2 = \$19 \]

Now, we add up the money Dora has:
\[ \text{Money saved: } \$2 \]
\[ \text{Money from mother: } \$16 \]
\[ \text{Total money Dora has: } \$2 + \$16 = \$18 \]

To find out how much more money Dora needs, we subtract the total money she has from the total cost of the items:
\[ \text{Total money Dora has: } \$18 \]
\[ \text{Total cost of items: } \$19 \]
\[ \text{Additional money needed: } \$19 - \$18 = \$1 \]

So, Dora needs an additional $1 to buy the skipping rope, the board game, and the playground ball.
